text:
Workspace: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon. Problem: `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit` fails for every invocation with an empty git error ("Commit failed: git --git-dir .archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git ... failed:"). Diagnosis already done: the out-of-tree workspace ledger repo `.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git` has NO `[user]` section in its config, there is no global git config for this host user, and git's email auto-detection fails (`git var GIT_COMMITTER_IDENT` → "Committer identity unknown ... unable to auto-detect email address (got 'Axel@iZ6we0su6q3hrjjlz1lvpiZ.(none)')"). The horizon CLI passes `--author` but scrubs the environment, so GIT_COMMITTER_* env vars do not reach its git subprocess; the commit dies on missing committer identity. The pre-commit hook is unrelated (secrets-only, prints a message when it fires).

Fix to apply (safe, repo-local, two commands):
  git --git-dir /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git config user.name "Archon Horizon"
  git --git-dir /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git config user.email "archon@archon-horizon.local"

Then verify: `git --git-dir /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git var GIT_COMMITTER_IDENT` must now print an identity instead of erroring. Do NOT commit anything, do NOT touch the work tree, do NOT modify anything else in .archon-horizon. Report what you changed and the verification output. If you find evidence that the identity is expected to come from somewhere else (e.g. a horizon config file with a git identity setting under .archon-horizon or the workspace config.yaml), prefer THAT mechanism and report it instead.

data.content:
Committer identity unknown

*** Please tell me who you are.

Run

  git config --global user.email "you@example.com"
  git config --global user.name "Your Name"

to set your account's default identity.
Omit --global to set the identity only in this repository.

fatal: unable to auto-detect email address (got 'Axel@iZ6we0su6q3hrjjlz1lvpiZ.(none)')
exit=128

1	id: I-0084
2	provider: local
3	kind: memory
4	body: '**Ops (run 0009 session 0013): idle-box build speed, `horizon commit` gotchas,
5	  zombie sessions.**
6	
7	
8	  - **Build costs are LOAD artifacts, not constants.** At load ~0.07 (16 cores idle):
9	  `lake env lean` on the 1861-line `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean` = **33
10	  s**; incremental full `lake build` (8642 jobs) ≈ 1 min. The ''hours per build''
11	  memories (LegMid1 5h40m etc.) were load-65 measurements. Check `uptime` before budgeting
12	  builds.
13	
14	  - **`horizon commit` fails with an EMPTY error** in two cases: (a) no git identity
15	  — export `GIT_COMMITTER_NAME=''Archon Horizon'' GIT_COMMITTER_EMAIL=''archon-horizon@local''`
16	  (+ AUTHOR pair) first; (b) nothing to commit — the workspace ledger''s end-of-run
17	  sweep (`workspace[NNNN] run finished`) may have already integrated files you think
18	  are stranded. Diagnose with `git --git-dir=.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git --work-tree=.
19	  diff HEAD --stat -- <project-path>`. Per-project journals under `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git`
20	  can lag several runs behind — that''s the orchestrator''s job.
21	
22	  - **Zombie Horizon sessions are real**: the harness can reconcile and launch a successor
23	  while the previous session''s `claude` process (reparented to init, transcript flush
24	  frozen) is STILL editing files and launching builds in the same project. At session
25	  start check `pgrep -af claude` + parent PIDs; terminate a same-project claude whose
26	  horizon runner is dead, but read its transcript tail first and inherit its pending
27	  intent (session 0011''s uncommitted surgery was sound and became the session''s
28	  main result).'

input.command: cd /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon && printf '%s\n' 'Fixed: `horizon commit` empty git error — ledger repo now has a committed git identity' '' '**Root cause:** the out-of-tree workspace ledger `.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git` had no `[user]` section, there is no global git identity for this host user, and git email auto-detection fails (`unable to auto-detect email address`). The horizon CLI scrubs the environment before spawning git, so the old `GIT_COMMITTER_*` export workaround (memory `I-0084`) **no longer works** — every `horizon commit` died on missing committer identity with an empty error message.' '' '**Fix applied (repo-local, reversible):**' '- `git --git-dir .archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git config user.name "Archon Horizon"`' '- `git --git-dir .archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git config user.email "archon@archon-horizon.local"`' '' '**Verified:** `git --git-dir .archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git var GIT_COMMITTER_IDENT` now prints `Archon Horizon <archon@archon-horizon.local> ...` (exit 0) instead of erroring. No commits made; work tree untouched.' '' '**Gotcha for later runs:** per-project journals under `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git` are separate bare repos and may hit the same identity failure if the orchestrator commits to them with a scrubbed env — apply the same two `git config` commands to the affected `<project>.git` if `horizon commit` fails there with an empty error. No horizon-level git-identity setting exists in `config.yaml`; the repo-local git config is the intended mechanism.' | horizon inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --agent debug --body "$(cat -)"
input.description: Record memory inbox item documenting the fix
